reasoning: The NFCI Leverage Subindex has shown a very steady and rapid upward momentum over the last 15 weeks, steadily rising from -0.558 in July 2025 to -0.202 on Oct 24, 2025 without a single down week. The typical change per week has accelerated to over +0.030. Given this exceptionally strong local momentum, the probability of the index being higher than -0.202 next week (Nov 2) is extremely high, and highly likely for the end of November (Nov 25). However, there appears to be strong seasonality for this index (which is explicitly not seasonally adjusted); in Q1 2025, the index plunged significantly from December 2024 to early February 2025. Extrapolating this seasonality suggests the index is likely to retreat by late January 2026, leading to a much lower probability of remaining above the October 2025 baseline over that longer horizon.
